attractions.name: Bartle Hall Convention Center (Kansas City Convention Center)
attractions.description: The Bartle Hall Convention Center is a premier event venue in Downtown Kansas City, featuring the world's largest column-free exhibit space. It is renowned for its striking architecture, including four 335-foot tall Art Deco pylons that support the roof over Interstate 670. The complex serves as a central hub for major conventions, trade shows, and entertainment in the region.
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed (Business Office), Sunday: Closed (Business Office). Event hours vary significantly by schedule.
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Business Office); Events scheduled daily.
attractions.admission: Admission varies by event; ticket prices and tiers are set by individual show organizers. Consult the official events calendar for specific pricing. Public areas like Barney Allis Plaza are free to access.
highlights: Features 388,800 square feet of contiguous, column-free exhibit space on a single floor.
Architectural landmark supported by four 335-foot concrete pylons topped with 'Sky Stations' sculptures.
Innovative design suspended over Interstate 670, integrating the facility into the city's skyline.
Connected to major downtown hotels and the Power & Light District via a network of skywalks.
LEED Silver certified, reflecting a commitment to sustainable building operations and green initiatives.
Part of a massive eight-square-block complex including a fine arts theater and a 10,700-seat arena.
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to reserve parking online in advance to secure a spot in the 1,000-space underground garage. For ticketed events, it is best to arrive 90 to 120 minutes early to allow for security screening. Utilize the climate-controlled skywalks to navigate comfortably between the convention center and nearby hotels during inclement weather.
attractions.nearby: Kansas City Power & Light District, Kauffman Center for the Performing Arts, T-Mobile Center, Municipal Auditorium, Barney Allis Plaza, and the Crossroads Arts District.

attractions.name: Folly Theater
attractions.description: The Folly Theater is Kansas City's oldest historic theater, originally opened in 1900 as the Standard Theater. This world-class performance venue is renowned for its exceptional acoustics and serves as a premier destination for jazz, children's series, and a diverse range of community events. Folly Theater, Wikipedia
attractions.region: Downtown Kansas City Loop / Central Business District
attractions.hours: Business Office: Monday - Friday, 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM. Theater Box Office: Typically opens 1 - 2 hours prior to show time. Folly Theater, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Business Office); Daily (Theater Box Office open 1-2 hours before shows). Folly Theater
attractions.admission: Varies by event (e.g., $17.00 - $22.00 for the Cappies Gala; service fees apply to all methods of purchase). Folly Theater, Folly Theater Tickets
highlights: Listed on the National Register of Historic Places. Folly Theater
Known as the "Grand Lady of 12th Street." Visit KC
Acoustics compared to Carnegie Hall. ArtsKC
Hosts the premier Folly Jazz Series and Kids Series. Wikipedia
Historical significance including past as a burlesque and vaudeville house. Wikipedia
attractions.tips: Arrive early as there is no full-service elevator to the balcony; all accessible seating is on the Orchestra level. Paid street parking and a nearby garage are available, but demand can be high during events. Folly Theater, American Theatre Guild
attractions.nearby: Barney Allis Plaza, Kansas City Public Library, Power and Light District, Kauffman Center for the Performing Arts, T-Mobile Center. Tripadvisor, Evendo

attractions.name: Kansas City Public Library - Central Library Official Site
attractions.category: Public Library, Landmark, Arts & Culture Wikipedia), Visit KC
attractions.description: The Kansas City Public Library's Central Branch is a magnificent community treasure housed in the restored 1906 First National Bank building. It serves as the system's main resource library, featuring breathtaking architecture like a 34-foot ceiling with a skylight and historic bank vaults repurposed for public use. Official Site, Visit KC
attractions.address: 14 West 10th Street, Kansas City, MO 64105 Visit KC, Official Site
attractions.region: Library District, Downtown Kansas City Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Mon-Wed: 9 AM - 7 PM; Thurs-Sat: 9 AM - 5 PM; Sun: 1 PM - 5 PM Official Site
attractions.admission: Free Official Site, Yelp
highlights: The Community Bookshelf: A striking parking garage facade featuring 25-foot-tall book spines of 22 literary classics. Wikipedia
Durwood Film Vault: A former bank vault converted into an intimate 28-seat movie theater showing free films. Official Site
Rooftop Terrace: An outdoor space featuring a life-size chess set and panoramic views of the downtown skyline. Visit KC
Missouri Valley Special Collections: A vast archive of local history, including historic postcards, maps, and photographs. Wikipedia)
Grand Reading Room: A stately space on the third floor modeled after early 20th-century library reading rooms. Official Site
attractions.tips: The library validates parking if you use the library's parking garage (the one with the giant bookshelf facade). Be sure to visit the basement to see the heavy bank vaults and the rooftop for a game of chess. Yelp, Visit KC
attractions.nearby: KC Streetcar, Arabia Steamboat Museum, City Market, Quality Hill Playhouse, and the Folly Theater. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: SEA LIFE Kansas City Aquarium
attractions.description: SEA LIFE Kansas City Aquarium is an immersive underwater experience home to over 5,000 sea creatures. Visitors can explore 11 themed exhibits, including Kansas City's only 180-degree underwater ocean tunnel, and encounter sharks, rescued sea turtles, and mesmerizing jellyfish.
attractions.address: 2475 Grand Blvd Suite 100, Kansas City, MO 64108
attractions.region: Crown Center District / Downtown Kansas City
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Last entry is one hour before closing) Official Website
attractions.admission: General Admission starting from $19.00 (Online), Admission + Virtual Reality + Digital Photos from $26.00, Combo Ticket (SEA LIFE + LEGOLAND Discovery Center) from $29.99, Annual Pass from $59.00. Children 2 and under are free. Official Website
highlights: Kansas City's only 180-degree underwater Ocean Tunnel
Interactive Rockpool where visitors can touch sea stars and pencil sea urchins
Rescued sea turtles in the Sea Turtle Rescue Center
Over 12 breathtaking exhibits including Stingray Bay and Jellies Exhibit
Doodle Reef where virtual sea creatures come to life
attractions.tips: Book tickets online in advance to save on admission prices. Validated free parking is available in the Crown Center Parking Garage. Check feeding times upon arrival to catch the shark or ray feeds. Sensory bags are available at admissions for a more inclusive experience. Official Website

attractions.name: West Bottoms (Historic West Bottoms 12th St Bridge Entertainment District)
attractions.description: The West Bottoms is a historic industrial neighborhood in Kansas City, Missouri, known for its massive brick warehouses, agricultural heritage, and vibrant revitalization. Originally the site of the city's stockyards and first union depot, it has evolved into a premier destination for antique shopping, arts, and seasonal haunted attractions. (West Bottoms, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 1401 W 12th St, Kansas City, MO 64101 (Historic West Bottoms 12th St Bridge Entertainment District central office)
attractions.hours: General Area: Open 24/7; First Friday Weekends: Friday 8:30 AM – Sunday 4:00 PM; Individual store/restaurant hours vary (many open weekends only) (West Bottoms)
attractions.days_open: Open daily for general exploration; Most shops and major events are active during "First Friday Weekends" (First weekend of every month) (West Bottoms)
attractions.admission: Free to explore the neighborhood; Individual attraction prices vary (e.g., Haunted Houses typically $25-$50+, Hy-Vee Arena varies by event) (West Bottoms, Visit KC)
highlights: First Friday Weekends: Monthly celebration with antique shopping, food trucks, and live music.
Haunted House District: Home to world-class seasonal haunts like Edge of Hell and The Beast.
Antique & Vintage Shopping: Numerous multi-level shops such as Bella Patina and Good Ju Ju.
Historic Architecture: Features the Kansas City Livestock Exchange Building and the 12th Street Bridge.
Culinary Scene: Eclectic dining including Chef J BBQ, The Campground, and Stockyards Brewing Co.
Hy-Vee Arena: A renovated sports complex and former home of the 1988 Final Four.
attractions.tips: Plan your visit for the First Friday weekend of the month to experience the district at its most active with the most shops open. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the area is expansive with many multi-story warehouses and industrial terrain. (Visit KC, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Kaw Point Park, Power & Light District, Crossroads Arts District, Riverfront Heritage Trail (Visit KC)

For over 60 years, it has served as a cultural catalyst, producing a diverse season of classics, musicals, and new works across two major venues. KCRep History

attractions.hours: Phone Hours: Monday – Friday, 10 AM – 4 PM (Extended until curtain on performance days). Box Office: Opens 1 hour before curtain on performance days. KCRep

attractions.admission: Ticket prices typically range from $69.00 to $109.00 per seat (plus a $5 processing fee), with some performances starting as low as $35.00 depending on the show and seating tier. KCRep Ticketing

attractions.name: T-Mobile Center
attractions.description: T-Mobile Center is a premier multi-purpose arena located in downtown Kansas City, serving as the region's primary venue for major concerts, sporting events, and family shows. Opened in 2007 as the Sprint Center, the glass-encased arena anchors the Power & Light District and has become a landmark of the city's urban revitalization. (Wikipedia, Visit KC)
attractions.hours: Box Office: Opens 3 hours prior to show time. Administrative Hours: Monday–Friday 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM. (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Open for scheduled events and weekday ticket sales. (T-Mobile Center)
attractions.admission: Varies by event (typically $25–$500+); connected College Basketball Experience: Adults (18-64) $20, Youth (4-17) $18, Seniors (65+) $15, Military $16, Kids 3 & under Free. (Official Website, College Basketball Experience)
highlights: Annual host of the Big 12 Men's Basketball Tournament.
Connected to the College Basketball Experience and National Collegiate Basketball Hall of Fame.
Features 'The Moons' public art installation by Chris Doyle.
Equipped with state-of-the-art 360-degree LED video screens and 5G technology.
Consistently ranked as one of the busiest arenas in the United States.
Exclusive amenities and fast-pass entry for T-Mobile customers.
attractions.tips: The arena is a completely cashless venue; bring credit or debit cards for all purchases. T-Mobile, Metro, and Mint customers can use an exclusive fast-pass entrance on 13th Street by showing their phone or Magenta Pass. (Official Website)
attractions.nearby: Kansas City Power & Light District, College Basketball Experience, National WWI Museum and Memorial, Union Station, Negro Leagues Baseball Museum. (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Diana Fountain (Diana and Cherubs Fountain) City of Fountains Foundation
attractions.description: The Diana Fountain is a prominent bronze sculpture depicting the Roman goddess of the moon, Diana, surrounded by cherubs. It is famously set against a grand 17-foot-tall, 56-foot-wide man-made waterfall that serves as a spectacular entrance to the Country Club Plaza district. City of Fountains Foundation, InterContinental Kansas City at the Plaza

attractions.city: Kansas City City of Fountains Foundation

attractions.admission: Free Visit KC

Backed by a massive 17-foot-tall waterfall with a 56-foot arc. City of Fountains Foundation
Located at the iconic intersection of Ward Parkway and Wornall Road. Visit KC
The fountain basin is frequently adorned with seasonal flowers and greenery. City of Fountains Foundation
Part of the Country Club Plaza's world-renowned collection of European-style fountains. City of Fountains Foundation
Beautifully illuminated at night, creating a romantic atmosphere. Visit KC
attractions.tips: The waterfall is best photographed from the opposite side of Ward Parkway for a full view. It is typically operational from April through late October, weather permitting. attractions.name: Frank A. Theis Park
attractions.description: Frank A. Theis Park is a prominent 14-acre green space located in the heart of Kansas City's cultural district. It features expansive lawns, a scenic jogging path along Brush Creek, and an amphitheatre frequently used for theatrical performances and community events. (KC Parks, Visit KC)
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Thursday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Friday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Saturday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Sunday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM (KC Parks)
attractions.admission: Free (KC Parks)

Large open lawn for picnics and games
Amphitheatre for theatrical performances and weddings
Scenic jogging and walking path along Brush Creek
Proximity to the Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art
Beautiful views of the Country Club Plaza skyline
attractions.tips: The park is an ideal spot for a picnic or dog walking, especially with its large open space. It is particularly scenic during events like the Water Lantern Festival or seasonal lawn parties. Visitors often combine a trip to the park with a visit to the adjacent Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art. (Yelp)
attractions.nearby: Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art, Country Club Plaza, Brush Creek, Kauffman Legacy Park, Anita B. Gorman Conservation Discovery Center (Yelp)

attractions.name: Jacob L. Loose Park
attractions.description: Jacob L. Loose Park is a historic 75-acre urban park located near the Country Club Plaza in Kansas City, Missouri. It is home to the renowned Laura Conyers Smith Municipal Rose Garden and served as a significant site during the Civil War Battle of Westport. The park offers visitors a peaceful retreat with its scenic lake, Japanese Tea Room, and extensive paved walking trails (Wikipedia, Kansas City Parks & Recreation).
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM (Go Wandering)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit KC, Trip.com)
highlights: Laura Conyers Smith Municipal Rose Garden featuring over 3,000 roses (Kansas City Parks & Recreation)
Historical site of the Civil War Battle of Westport with commemorative markers (Wikipedia)
Japanese Tea Room and Garden representing a cultural exchange with Kurashiki, Japan (Wikipedia)
Lake Drummond (duck pond) with a central fountain and arched bridges (KC Hiker)
Paved 1-mile walking loop trail providing views of the park and historic homes (KC Hiker)
Large playground area and reservable picnic shelters for community gatherings (Go Wandering)
attractions.tips: Visit during June to see the rose garden at its peak bloom; keep in mind that the parking lot gate closes at 10 PM even though the park is open until midnight (Go Wandering). Permits are required for weddings and professional photography sessions (Kansas City Parks & Recreation).
attractions.nearby: Country Club Plaza shopping district, Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art, and Ewing and Muriel Kauffman Memorial Garden (Visit KC, Reddit)

attractions.name: Kansas City Board of Trade
attractions.description: The Kansas City Board of Trade was a premier commodity futures and options exchange, most famous for its hard red winter wheat contracts. Established in 1856, it played a central role in the global agricultural economy for over 150 years until its acquisition by the CME Group in 2012. Today, its iconic former headquarters at 4800 Main Street stands as a landmark building housing various commercial offices and retail establishments. (Wikipedia, CME Group)
attractions.hours: Mon: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Tue: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Wed: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Thu: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Fri: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Sat: Closed, Sun: Closed (Veterans Affairs)
attractions.admission: Free (Wikipedia)
highlights: World's largest futures market for hard red winter wheat contracts (CME Group)
First exchange in the world to offer a stock index futures contract in 1982 (Wikipedia)
Historic headquarters designed specifically for grain exchange operations (Wikipedia)
Acquired by the Chicago-based CME Group for $126 million in 2012 (Wikipedia)
Significant contributor to Kansas City's development as a major agricultural hub (Pendergast Years)
attractions.tips: While the trading floor is no longer operational, visitors can still admire the building's architecture and visit ground-floor retail and restaurants like Stock Hill or Banksia. Check for historical markers in the South Plaza area to learn more about the district's trading heritage. (LoopNet, NSDKC)
attractions.nearby: Country Club Plaza, Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art, Kansas City Public Library - Plaza Branch (Wikipedia, Yelp)

attractions.description: Stock Hill is an award-winning steakhouse that reimagines the classic dining experience for a new generation. Nestled in the historic former Kansas City Board of Trade building, it combines impeccable craftsmanship and imaginative interpretations of steakhouse staples with a sophisticated, two-story atmosphere.

AAA Four Diamond Award winner from 2017 to 2023.

Located in the historic 14,000-square-foot former Board of Trade building.
Includes a unique 'Social Hour' with daily specials in the lounge and bar areas.

Note that an auto-gratuity of 20% is added for groups of seven or larger, and the restaurant does not split checks. Every Thursday, the restaurant offers a 'Girl Dinner' special in the lounge and bar areas.

attractions.name: University of Missouri–Kansas City (UMKC)
attractions.description: The University of Missouri-Kansas City (UMKC) is a public research university and the largest in the Kansas City metropolitan area. As a Carnegie R1 institution, it offers more than 125 degree programs across 12 academic units, including Missouri's only public dental school. The university is spread across multiple campuses, including the historic Volker Campus and the Health Sciences District on Hospital Hill. Wikipedia
attractions.region: Rockhill / Country Club Plaza area Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Mon: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Tue: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Wed: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Thu: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Fri: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM, Sat: Closed (Offices), Sun: Closed (Offices) UMKC Admissions
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Campus); Monday - Friday (Offices) UMKC Admissions
attractions.admission: Free (General campus access and prospective student tours) UMKC Admissions

Home to Missouri’s only public dental school Wikipedia
Features the iconic Kasey Kangaroo mascot, originally drawn by Walt Disney Wikipedia
Houses KCUR 89.3, the region’s primary NPR affiliate Wikipedia
Strong community ties through the UMKC Health Sciences District on Hospital Hill Wikipedia
Located adjacent to major cultural landmarks like the Country Club Plaza Wikipedia
attractions.tips: Prospective students should schedule a 'Roo Visit' for a student-led walking tour of the Volker Campus. Be sure to explore the historic Quad, which features some of the university's oldest and most iconic buildings like Scofield and Newcomb Halls. A virtual tour is also available online for those unable to visit in person. UMKC Admissions

attractions.name: Arthur Bryant's Barbeque (Official Website)
attractions.description: Arthur Bryant’s Barbeque is a legendary Kansas City institution, often cited as one of the most famous barbecue restaurants in the United States. Founded in 1908 and relocated to its iconic 18th & Brooklyn site in 1958, the restaurant is world-renowned for its slow-smoked meats, unique secret-recipe sauces, and for being the birthplace of the barbecue delicacy known as "burnt ends." (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 1727 Brooklyn Avenue, Kansas City, MO 64127 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Saturday: 11:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 7:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Credited with originating and popularizing "burnt ends," a staple of Kansas City barbecue culture. ([Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arthur_Bryant\
father of Kansas City barbecue,
single best restaurant in the world
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid long lunch and weekend lines; the restaurant features a traditional, no-frills cafeteria-style service. Portions are notably large, especially the sandwiches served on plain white bread, and the lard-fried hand-cut fries and baked beans are essential sides to try. (Much to be Done, The Cravings Queen)
attractions.nearby: Negro Leagues Baseball Museum, American Jazz Museum, Historic 18th & Vine Jazz District, Gem Theater (Much to be Done)

attractions.name: Negro Leagues Baseball Museum
attractions.description: The Negro Leagues Baseball Museum is the world’s only national museum dedicated to preserving and celebrating the rich history of African-American baseball and its impact on the social advancement of America. Established in 1990, it features a self-guided tour through a chronological timeline of baseball history, from its origins after the Civil War to its integration into the major leagues. (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Tuesday–Saturday: 10:00 am – 5:00 pm; Sunday: 12:00 pm – 5:00 pm; Monday: Closed (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults: $10.00; Seniors (65+): $9.00; Kids (5–12): $6.00; Kids (0–4): Free; Active Military: Free; Museum Members: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Field of Legends: A centerpiece exhibit featuring life-size bronze statues of Negro League legends on a replica baseball diamond (Official Website)
Chronological Timeline: A detailed history of Black baseball integrated with American social history and the Civil Rights movement (Official Website)
Negro Leagues Béisbol: An exhibit exploring the connections between American and Latin baseball history (Official Website)
Extra Innings Gift Shop: Features officially licensed Negro Leagues merchandise (Official Website)
Film Exhibits and Artifacts: Multimedia presentations and hundreds of historical photographs and artifacts (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Allow at least one to two hours to fully experience the self-guided tour. Photography is generally not permitted within the exhibits. Visitors can check luggage at the main security desk. It is recommended to purchase tickets online in advance. (Official Website, Visit KC)
attractions.nearby: American Jazz Museum (shared building), Historic 18th & Vine District, Arthur Bryant's Barbeque, The Rabbit Hole, J. Rieger & Co. (Visit KC, Reddit)

Located in the heart of historic Westport, it offers a laid-back, urban hip atmosphere with a menu featuring everything from Belgian-style fries and artisan burgers to a legendary made-from-scratch weekend brunch (Beer Kitchen, Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: McCoy's Public House
attractions.description: McCoy's Public House was a landmark Westport brewery and restaurant known for its handcrafted beers and American comfort food with a creative twist. Established in 1997, it served as a central social hub in the historic district for over 21 years before closing in late 2018 (Flatland KC, Kansas City Star).

attractions.tips: Try the Mac and Cheese or the 'Real McCoy' burger; the patio is highly recommended for those with pets or looking for a lively outdoor atmosphere (Tripadvisor, The Red Tape Chronicles).

attractions.name: The Arabia Steamboat Museum
attractions.description: The Arabia Steamboat Museum is a unique time capsule of life on the American frontier in the mid-19th century, housing 200 tons of cargo salvaged from a steamboat that sank in 1856. Voted a 'Favorite Kansas City Hidden Gem,' it features the largest single collection of pre-Civil War artifacts in the world, ranging from fine china and clothing to the world's oldest pickles. The Arabia Steamboat Museum

attractions.name: Kansas City Union Station
attractions.description: Kansas City Union Station is a historic landmark and vibrant cultural hub that originally opened in 1914 as a major rail hub. Today, it houses a variety of world-class attractions including Science City, the Arvin Gottlieb Planetarium, and rotating exhibitions, while continuing to serve as an active Amtrak station. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 30 West Pershing Road, Kansas City, MO 64108
attractions.region: Downtown / Crown Center area Visit KC
attractions.hours: Union Station Building: Monday–Sunday 6:00 AM – 12:00 AM; Science City: Tuesday–Saturday 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sunday 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM, Monday Closed Official Website
attractions.days_open: Daily (Union Station Building); Science City: Tuesday–Sunday (Closed Mondays) Official Website
attractions.admission: Union Station Building & Model Train Gallery: Free; Science City: $17.25 (General), Free (Children 12 months and under); Arvin Gottlieb Planetarium: $8.00; Regnier Extreme Screen: $8.00 (Adults), $7.00 (Ages 3-11) Official Website
highlights: Science City: An award-winning interactive science center with over 300 hands-on exhibits.
Arvin Gottlieb Planetarium: The region's largest planetarium offering immersive astronomy shows.
Model Train Gallery: An 8,000-square-foot exhibit featuring intricate model railroading displays.
Regnier Extreme Screen Theatre: A massive five-story-tall screen for documentaries and feature films.
Grand Hall: A majestic space featuring 95-foot ceilings and three 3,500-pound chandeliers.
Amtrak Station: A functioning rail hub for the Missouri River Runner and Southwest Chief lines.
attractions.tips: Take the free KC Streetcar for convenient access from downtown; book attraction tickets online in advance to ensure entry; use the climate-controlled skybridge (The Link) to walk to Crown Center attractions; and check out the Model Train Gallery for a free volunteer-led experience. Official Website, Visit KC
attractions.nearby: National WWI Museum and Memorial, Crown Center, SEA LIFE Kansas City, LEGOLAND Discovery Center, Hallmark Visitor Center Tripadvisor

attractions.name: National World War I Museum and Memorial (Liberty Memorial)
attractions.description: The National World War I Museum and Memorial is America's leading institution dedicated to remembering and understanding the Great War. It features the iconic 217-foot Liberty Memorial Tower and houses one of the most comprehensive collections of WWI artifacts in the world (Wikipedia, National WWI Museum).
attractions.region: Penn Valley Park / Crown Center (National Park Service)

attractions.days_open: Wednesday through Monday (Regular); Daily (Summer: Memorial Day to Labor Day) (National WWI Museum)
attractions.admission: General Admission: Adult $19.50, Senior (65+) $15.50, Youth (6-15) $11.50, Military $15.50, Children (Under 6) Free. Combo (GA + Tower + Exhibition): Adult $26.50, Senior $22.50, Youth $18.50, Military $22.50, Children Free. Grounds: Free (National WWI Museum)
highlights: Liberty Memorial Tower for panoramic 360-degree views of Kansas City (National WWI Museum)
Paul Sunderland Glass Bridge over a field of 9,000 silk poppies (TourismAttractions)
Immersive trench warfare recreations and walkthrough displays (KCUR)
One of the world's largest collections of WWI artillery and artifacts (Wikipedia)
Over There Café offering WWI-themed dining experiences (National WWI Museum)
attractions.tips: Allow 3-4 hours for a full visit. The tower requires climbing 45 stairs after the elevator ride. Parking is free in the south lot. Tickets are typically valid for two consecutive days (TourismAttractions).

attractions.name: Country Club Plaza
attractions.description: Country Club Plaza is a historic open-air shopping and dining destination in the heart of Kansas City, established in 1922 and stretching across 15 city blocks. The district features stunning Spanish-inspired architecture with red tile roofs, intricate tilework, and lush courtyards, serving as a unique cultural and retail landmark. It is home to over 120 shops and 30 restaurants, offering a blend of luxury brands, local boutiques, and exquisite dining options (Official Website).
attractions.address: 4706 Broadway Blvd, Kansas City, MO 64112 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday-Saturday: 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit KC)
highlights: Spanish-inspired architecture modeled after Seville, Spain (Wikipedia)
Over 75 unique statues, fountains, and pieces of one-of-a-kind artwork (Tripadvisor)
Host of the annual Plaza Lights, a tradition where buildings are outlined in holiday lights from Thanksgiving to mid-January (Official Website)
Home to more than 120 shops and 30 restaurants (Tripadvisor)
First planned regional shopping center in the United States designed for shoppers arriving by car (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Parking is free year-round in several garages throughout the district. The area is best explored on foot to fully appreciate the architecture and statuary. Consider visiting during the holiday season to experience the iconic Plaza Lights (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: J.C. Nichols Memorial Fountain, Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art, Loose Park, Kemper Museum of Contemporary Art, Mill Creek Park (Tripadvisor)

attractions.description: Gates Bar-B-Q is a family-owned Kansas City original founded in 1946, celebrated as a pillar of Kansas City-style barbecue. It is world-famous for its signature "Hi, May I Help You?" greeting, iconic red-roof architecture, and the tuxedo-clad "Struttin' Man" logo. Gates Bar-B-Q History, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Thomas Hart Benton Home and Studio State Historic Site
attractions.description: The Thomas Hart Benton Home and Studio State Historic Site preserves the 1903 late-Victorian residence and carriage house studio where the world-renowned Regionalist artist lived and worked for 36 years. Visitors can explore the home filled with original possessions and the studio where Benton's paintbrushes and jars of paint remain exactly as they were when he passed away in 1975. Missouri State Parks, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Year-round: Mon, Thu-Sat: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Sun: 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Tue-Wed: Closed. Missouri State Parks
attractions.days_open: Monday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Closed Tuesday and Wednesday)
attractions.admission: Adults (18+): $5.00, Youth (6-17): $3.50, Children (5 and under): Free. Group rates (15+ people) are $4.00 for adults and $3.00 for students. Missouri State Parks, Teacher's Guide
highlights: Preserved carriage house art studio containing Benton's original paintbrushes and supplies. Missouri State Parks

attractions.tips: Guided tours are the only way to see the house and studio; reservations are highly recommended and can be made online. Parking is limited to street-side in the residential neighborhood, and while the first floor and studio are ADA accessible, a photo flipbook is provided for those unable to access the second floor. attractions.category: Museum, Landmark, Historic Site Bingham-Waggoner Mansion & Estate
attractions.description: The Bingham-Waggoner Mansion & Estate is a beautifully restored 26-room Victorian mansion sitting on a 19.5-acre estate in the heart of Independence. It was once the home of famous 19th-century artist George Caleb Bingham and the Waggoner family, who occupied the residence for 97 years. Today, it serves as a museum and park dedicated to preserving the history of the westward trails. Bingham-Waggoner Mansion & Estate, City of Independence

attractions.name: 18th and Vine Historic Jazz District
attractions.category: Historic District, Cultural District, Entertainment, Museum
attractions.description: The 18th and Vine Historic Jazz District is the internationally recognized cradle of Kansas City jazz and a historic hub of African-American culture. Developed during the era of segregation, it flourished into a thriving neighborhood known for its vibrant nightlife, legendary barbecue, and as the home of jazz icons like Charlie Parker and Count Basie. Wikipedia, AAHTKC
attractions.hours: Museum Hours: Mon: Closed, Tue-Sat: 10 AM - 5 PM, Sun: 12 PM - 5 PM; Mutual Musicians Foundation: Fri-Sat: 12 AM - 5 AM; District: 24/7 Access to outdoor monuments. American Jazz Museum, Visit KC
attractions.days_open: District: 7 days a week; Museums: Tuesday – Sunday. American Jazz Museum, Kansas City Star
attractions.admission: District access is Free; American Jazz Museum: Adults $10, Seniors (60+) $9, Youth (6-18) $6, Children under 5 Free, Military $4, Students/Educators $8; Combo Ticket (with Negro Leagues Baseball Museum): $15. American Jazz Museum, YouTube
highlights: American Jazz Museum and Negro Leagues Baseball Museum complex
Charlie Parker Memorial (iconic 17-foot bronze sculpture)

The Blue Room, a working jazz club and museum exhibit

Legendary Kansas City barbecue at Arthur Bryant’s
attractions.tips: The district is compact and very walkable; purchase a combo ticket to visit both the Jazz and Negro Leagues Baseball Museums at a discount. For a truly authentic experience, visit the Mutual Musicians Foundation after midnight on weekends for legendary jam sessions. Kansas City Star, Visit KC

attractions.name: Baker University
attractions.description: Founded in 1858, Baker University is a private liberal arts institution and the first four-year university established in Kansas. It offers over 50 degree programs across four schools, including a dedicated campus in the Kansas City area for adult and graduate studies. The university is recognized for its academic rigor, historic traditions, and affiliation with the United Methodist Church (Baker University, Wikipedia).

attractions.region: Johnson County / Kansas City Metropolitan Area
attractions.hours: Monday – Thursday: 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM, Friday: 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday – Sunday: Closed (Waze, Overland Park Chamber).

highlights: First four-year university in the state of Kansas, chartered in 1858 (Baker University).
Offers over 50 undergraduate and graduate programs, including master's and doctoral degrees (Baker University).

Home to a highly ranked School of Nursing operated in partnership with Stormont Vail Health (Baker University).
The main Baldwin City campus includes three buildings listed on the National Register of Historic Places (Baker University).
attractions.tips: The Overland Park location is an urban campus primarily serving adult and graduate students; visitors seeking the university's historic architecture and museums should visit the residential campus in Baldwin City, located about 40 minutes southwest (Baker University).

The company is celebrated for its blend of traditional brewing techniques and modern innovation, as well as its commitment to sustainability. Official Site, Visit KC

It is celebrated for its charming Tudor-style architecture, walkable streets, and a vibrant collection of over 90 local shops, restaurants, and service businesses. The area maintains a unique small-town feel while serving as a central hub for the surrounding residential community. (Brookside Business Association, Wikipedia)

attractions.description: The Crossroads Arts District is a vibrant, creative neighborhood in downtown Kansas City, renowned for its concentration of more than 400 local artists and 100 independent studios kccrossroads.org. Features 25-foot ceilings and a fireplace lounge in a historic 1800s freight house (Visit KC)
Uses a unique wood-fired cookery with 100% natural Ozark Hickory flavor (Yelp)
Offers one of the most extensive barbecue menus including certified Angus steaks and seafood (Jack Stack Barbecue)
Includes private dining options like the Zephyr Room for intimate gatherings (Jack Stack Barbecue)
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended due to high demand and long waits, especially on weekends (Tripadvisor). Try the world-famous cheesy corn bake and hickory pit beans as side dishes (Yelp).
attractions.nearby: Union Station, Science City, National WWI Museum and Memorial, Crossroads Art District (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Fort Osage National Historic Landmark
attractions.description: Built in 1808 under the direction of William Clark, Fort Osage served as both a military garrison and a factory trade center on the American Frontier. Today, it is a partially reconstructed historic site where authentically attired interpreters provide a living history experience of early 19th-century military, civilian, and Native American life (Jackson County Parks, Wikipedia).
attractions.region: Kansas City Metropolitan Area / Jackson County
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Sunday: 09:00 AM – 04:30 PM; Monday: Closed (Jackson County Parks)
attractions.admission: Adults: $8, Children (5-13): $4, Seniors (62 and over): $4, Children under 5: Free (Jackson County Parks, Fort Osage NHS)
highlights: Reconstructed historic fort buildings overlooking the Missouri River (Jackson County Parks)
Authentically attired historical interpreters demonstrating daily frontier life (Tripadvisor)
Fort Osage Education Center featuring 4,000 square feet of museum exhibits (Jackson County Parks)
Direct connections to the Lewis and Clark and Santa Fe National Historic Trails (Jackson County Parks)
Living history events and workshops, such as the annual Fall Muster (Our Changing Lives)
On-site Education Center Gift Store with historic reproductions and unique gifts (Jackson County Parks)
attractions.tips: Allow 1-2 hours for a full tour of the fort and museum exhibits. Wear comfortable walking shoes for the outdoor terrain and check the event calendar for special living history demonstrations. Non-commercial photography is permitted (Jackson County Parks, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Bonehill View Distillery, Heisler-burns Park, Cooley Lake Conservation Area, Harry S. Truman Presidential Library & Museum, National Frontier Trails Museum (Tripadvisor, Lewis and Clark Trail Experience)

attractions.name: GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium
attractions.description: GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium is the iconic home of the NFL's Kansas City Chiefs and is renowned for its electric atmosphere and record-breaking crowd noise. As part of the Truman Sports Complex, it serves as a premier destination for world-class sports and entertainment, including major concerts and upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup matches Visit KC.
attractions.hours: Public Tours: Friday & Saturday (times vary by booking). Private Tours: Sunday - Thursday, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (non-event days). Main Office: Monday - Friday, 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium, NFL.com.
attractions.days_open: Tours offered daily (Public on Friday/Saturday, Private Sunday-Thursday); Closed on gamedays and days following home games GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium.
attractions.admission: Public Tours: Adult $40, Senior (65+) $35, Child (5-12) $35, Military $35, Children 4 & under Free GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium. Private Tours: Start at $75 per person Tripadvisor.
highlights: Home to the NFL's Kansas City Chiefs and a 2026 FIFA World Cup host venue Kansas City FWC26
Guinness World Record holder for the loudest outdoor stadium crowd noise KC Yesterday
Features the Chiefs Hall of Honor, showcasing franchise history and memorabilia GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium
Part of the Truman Sports Complex alongside Kauffman Stadium Yahoo Sports
Offers behind-the-scenes guided tours of the locker rooms, press box, and field sidelines GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium
attractions.tips: Purchase parking passes online in advance as they are not sold at the gate; the stadium is a completely cashless venue; and strictly follow the NFL Clear Bag Policy for all events Reddit, GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium.
attractions.nearby: Kauffman Stadium (home of the KC Royals), Truman Sports Complex, Cave Spring Park, and various local BBQ favorites like Dixon's Chili Parlor Yahoo Sports, Visit KC.

attractions.name: George E. Kessler Park (formerly North Terrace Park)
attractions.description: Spanning over 300 acres in Kansas City’s historic Northeast neighborhood, Kessler Park (formerly North Terrace Park) is a cornerstone of the city's parks and boulevard system. The park features dramatic natural topography with limestone bluffs overlooking the Missouri River valley and serves as a major regional destination for hiking, biking, and nature appreciation (Wikipedia), Kansas City Parks & Recreation).
attractions.address: 450 The Paseo, Kansas City, MO 64106 / 5200 Gladstone Blvd, Kansas City, MO 64120
attractions.hours: 5:00 a.m. – Midnight daily (Kansas City Parks & Recreation)
attractions.admission: Free (Kansas City Parks & Recreation)
highlights: Cliff Drive State Scenic Byway, a 4.5-mile non-motorized road through the park's wooded landscape (KC Parks)
The Colonnade, a historic 1908 Beaux-Arts pavilion and vista (Wikipedia))
Carl J. DiCapo Fountain, a picturesque 40-foot artificial waterfall (KCUR)
A 21-hole disc golf course situated around the abandoned Northeast Reservoir (UDisc)
Extensive network of natural-surface mountain biking and hiking trails (Urban Trail Co.)
Designated limestone climbing areas for bouldering and top-roping (Wikipedia))
attractions.tips: Cliff Drive is currently closed to motorized vehicles every day, making it an ideal sanctuary for pedestrians and cyclists. Visitors should check out the abandoned reservoir ruins for impressive graffiti art, but should be mindful of park hours and potential slippery conditions on trails (KCUR, Abandoned Missouri).
attractions.nearby: Kansas City Museum, Berkley Riverfront, Arabia Steamboat Museum, American Jazz Museum, Negro Leagues Baseball Museum (KCUR, Evendo)

attractions.name: Harry S. Truman Presidential Library & Museum
attractions.description: The Harry S. Truman Presidential Library & Museum preserves the papers, books, and historical materials of the 33rd U.S. President. It recently completed a massive $29 million renovation, the largest since it opened in 1957, featuring state-of-the-art interactive exhibits. (Official Site) (Visit KC)
attractions.hours: Monday – Saturday: 9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Sundays (April – December): 12:00 p.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Sundays (January – March): Closed (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Adults: $12; Seniors (62+): $10; Veterans/Military (retired-disabled): $10; College Students (with ID): $8; Youth (13-18): $5; Members and Children (12 and under): Free (Official Site)
highlights: The "An Ordinary Man, His Extraordinary Journey" permanent exhibit detailing Truman\
, "A life-size replica of the Oval Office as it appeared during Truman
The Truman Courtyard, which serves as the final resting place for President Harry Truman, First Lady Bess Truman, and their daughter.
Extensive collections of over 186,000 photographs and 5,000 sound recordings.
Interactive displays on the Cold War, the decision to use the atomic bomb, and the recognition of the State of Israel.
attractions.tips: Free parking is available in lots surrounding the building. Visitors must pass through a security screening upon entry, and flash photography is prohibited in exhibit areas. (Official Site)
attractions.nearby: Harry S Truman National Historic Site (Truman Home), Independence Square, National Frontier Trails Museum (NPS)

attractions.name: Historic Truman Courthouse (Jackson County Courthouse)
attractions.description: The Historic Truman Courthouse is a colonial revival landmark in Independence, Missouri, where Harry S. Truman began his political career as a presiding judge. Originally built in 1838 and significantly remodeled in the 1930s, it now serves as the home of the Jackson County Historical Society and the Jackson County Museum of Art. Jackson County, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday: 08:00 AM–05:00 PM, Tuesday: 08:00 AM–05:00 PM, Wednesday: 08:00 AM–05:00 PM, Thursday: 08:00 AM–05:00 PM, Friday: 08:00 AM–05:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed Jackson County
attractions.admission: Free (Guided tours were historically offered for free, but currently self-guided exterior viewing is standard; contact JCHS for interior tour availability) Jackson County, Tripadvisor
highlights: Harry S. Truman’s restored 1930s courtroom and office
Jackson County Historical Society History Center and Archives
Jackson County Museum of Art featuring George Caleb Bingham prints
Official starting point for the Santa Fe, Oregon, and California Trails
Statues of Presidents Harry S. Truman and Andrew Jackson on the grounds
Architectural window showing original 19th-century molding
attractions.tips: Check with the Jackson County Historical Society (JCHS) in advance if you wish to tour the interior, as regular public tours may be limited. The grounds feature numerous historical markers about westward expansion that are accessible anytime. JCHS, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Independence Square, Harry S. Truman National Historic Site, 1859 Jail Museum, National Frontier Trails Museum, Clinton's Soda Fountain Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Ilus W. Davis Park
attractions.description: Ilus W. Davis Park is a 5.2-acre urban green space in the heart of Downtown Kansas City, serving as a scenic "civic mall" between City Hall and the U.S. District Courthouse. The park features lush landscaping with over 350 trees, a large shallow reflecting pool, and a step-style fountain that provides a tranquil retreat amidst the city's government buildings. It is home to several significant monuments, including a nine-foot bronze statue of former Mayor Ilus Davis and the Bill of Rights monument.
attractions.hours: Monday: 5 AM-12 AM, Tuesday: 5 AM-12 AM, Wednesday: 5 AM-12 AM, Thursday: 5 AM-12 AM, Friday: 5 AM-12 AM, Saturday: 5 AM-12 AM, Sunday: 5 AM-12 AM
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Large shallow reflecting pool and cascading step-style fountain
Nine-foot bronze statue of Mayor Ilus Winfield Davis in the southwest corner
The Bill of Rights Monument located in the northeast corner of the park
The City Employees Memorial honoring workers who lost their lives in the line of duty
More than 350 trees, including flowering crab, red oaks, and ginkgoes
Scenic walking paths offering views of the surrounding Kansas City skyline
attractions.tips: Visit during the early morning or late afternoon for the best photography lighting of the skyline. The park is a popular spot for downtown office workers' lunches, so it's best enjoyed as a quiet picnic destination. It is easily accessible via the KC Streetcar and the MAX bus route.
attractions.nearby: Observation Deck at City Hall, The College Basketball Experience, T-Mobile Center, Arabia Steamboat Museum, Kansas City Public Library, and various historic downtown landmarks.

attractions.name: Jesse James Birthplace Museum (Official Website)
attractions.description: The Jesse James Birthplace is the historic family homestead where the legendary American outlaw Jesse James was born and raised. Visitors can explore the authentically preserved 1822 cabin, view the world's largest collection of James family artifacts, and see the site of Jesse's original grave. (Official Website, Visit Clay MO)
attractions.address: 21216 Jesse James Farm Rd, Kearney, Missouri 64060 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: June – August: 9am – 4pm daily; Rest of the year: 9am – 4pm Monday-Saturday, 12pm – 4pm Sunday; Closed Holidays (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Daily (June–August); Monday–Sunday (Rest of the year); Closed on all holidays (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults: $10.00; Seniors (62+): $9.00; Children (8-15): $6.00; Children Under 8: FREE; Group Rates (15+): Adults $9.00, Seniors $8.00, Children $5.00 (Official Website)
highlights: Guided tours of the original 1822 family cabin and farmhouse (Visit KC)
World's largest collection of James family artifacts, including Jesse's boots and personal letters (SavingPlaces)
Jesse James’ original burial site where his mother once sold souvenir stones (Visit Clay MO)
Informative 20-minute film recapping the history of the Frank and Jesse James gang (Official Website)
The 'Burn Marks' on the floor from a Pinkerton detective raid in 1875 (YouTube)
A picturesque paved trail leading from the museum to the historic farmhouse (Visit Clay MO)
attractions.tips: Photography is allowed in most areas for personal use, but flash and tri-pods are prohibited inside the museum. No tickets are needed in advance as they are sold on-site, and visitors should allow 1-2 hours for the full tour. (Official Website)
attractions.nearby: Watkins Mill State Park (Historic woolen mill and park), Jesse James Bank Museum in Liberty (Site of the first daylight bank robbery), Mt. Olivet Cemetery (Jesse's final resting place) (Visit Clay MO, Atlas Obscura)

attractions.name: Joe's Kansas City Bar-B-Que
attractions.description: Joe's Kansas City Bar-B-Que is an iconic institution celebrated for its exceptional barbecue and its unique original location inside a working gas station. Founded in 1996, it has achieved worldwide fame and was named by Anthony Bourdain as one of the \"13 Places You Must Eat Before You Die.\" (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday-Thursday: 11:00 a.m. - 9:00 p.m.; Friday-Saturday: 11:00 a.m. - 10:00 p.m.; Sunday: CLOSED (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: The Z-Man Sandwich: A legendary combination of slow-smoked brisket, smoked provolone cheese, and crispy onion rings on a Kaiser roll (Official Website).
The Original Gas Station: The restaurant's flagship location remains a fully functional gas station, adding to its unique charm and history (Official Website).
Burnt Ends: Highly sought-after smoked meat delicacies available primarily on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Saturdays (Official Website).
Anthony Bourdain Endorsement: Famously recognized by the late chef as a bucket-list dining destination (Wikipedia).
The Rocket Pig: A popular specialty sandwich featuring pulled pork tossed in a sweet-hot competition glaze with bacon and pepper jack (Official Website).
attractions.tips: Expect long lines that often wrap around the building; arriving before the 11 a.m. opening is recommended. If you want burnt ends, be sure to visit on their specific serving days (typically Mon/Wed/Sat) as they sell out fast. You can fill up your gas tank at the same location before or after your meal (Tripadvisor, Visit KC).
attractions.nearby: Westwood Spa, Gus's World Famous Hot & Spicy Fried Chicken, 1889 Pizza Napoletana, Loose Park, Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art, Country Club Plaza (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Joe's Kansas City Bar-B-Que (formerly Oklahoma Joe's)
attractions.description: Joe's Kansas City Bar-B-Que is an iconic barbecue institution famously located within a working gas station. Originally established in 1996 as Oklahoma Joe's, it has earned worldwide acclaim for its high-quality smoked meats and unique atmosphere. It is widely considered one of the must-visit culinary destinations in the Kansas City area.
attractions.hours: Monday-Thursday: 11:00 a.m. - 9:00 p.m.; Friday-Saturday: 11:00 a.m. - 10:00 p.m.; Sunday: CLOSED
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant)
highlights: The Z-Man Sandwich: slow-smoked beef brisket, smoked provolone cheese, and two crispy onion rings on a toasted Kaiser roll.
Famous seasoned French fries, which have earned a cult following for their unique blend of spices.
Authentic "gas station" atmosphere at the original location, complete with working fuel pumps.
Multiple honors from celebrity chefs and publications, including Anthony Bourdain\
13 Places to Eat Before You Die.
attractions.tips: Lines are common, especially during lunch and dinner hours, so arriving early is recommended. The gas station location is the original and most famous, but other locations in Olathe and Leawood may have shorter wait times. Don't forget to try the Z-Man sandwich, the restaurant's most iconic menu item.
attractions.nearby: Lulu's Asian Bistro, Blue Sushi Sake Grill, Westwood Park, and The Escape Game Kansas City.

attractions.name: Kansas City Art Institute (KCAI) (Official Website)
attractions.description: Founded in 1885, the Kansas City Art Institute (KCAI) is one of the nation’s oldest and most respected colleges of art and design. This premier private, fully accredited four-year institution offers Bachelor of Fine Arts degrees in 13 majors and serves as an immersive studio environment where students prepare to creatively transform the world through art and design (KCAI About).
attractions.hours: Artspace: Wed-Sat 12-5 p.m. (Tue by appointment, Sun-Mon closed); KCAI Gallery: Thu-Sun 12-5 p.m. (KCAI Artspace, KCAI Gallery)
attractions.admission: Free (The Emily & Todd Voth Artspace and KCAI Gallery are free and open to the public) (KCAI Artspace, KCAI Gallery)
highlights: Home to the Emily & Todd Voth Artspace and KCAI Gallery, featuring innovative contemporary exhibitions (KCAI Artspace).
Strategically located between the Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art and the Kemper Museum of Contemporary Art (KCAI About).
Hosts signature community events like Road Roller Day, featuring large-scale woodblock prints created with construction equipment (KCAI Instagram).
Features a beautiful 16-acre campus with historic landmarks such as Vanderslice Hall and Mineral Hall (KCAI About, Wikipedia).
Offers 13 B.F.A. majors with a low 9:1 student-teacher ratio and 100% practicing artist faculty (KCAI About).
Features Marble & Canvas, a campus retail destination showcasing original works by students, alumni, and faculty (KCAI Instagram).
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to walk or bike through the 16-acre campus; bike rental stations are available just a block away. If arriving via the KC Streetcar, follow the 'Arterie' pathway from the Museums stop for a guided walk past the institute and neighboring museums (KCAI About, Nelson-Atkins Visit).
attractions.nearby: Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art, Kemper Museum of Contemporary Art, Country Club Plaza, and the historic Westport neighborhood (KCAI About, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Kansas City Power & Light District
attractions.description: The Kansas City Power & Light District is a vibrant nine-block dining, shopping, office, entertainment, and lifestyle neighborhood in the heart of downtown Kansas City. Since opening in 2008, it has served as a premier destination for world-class dining, award-winning entertainment, and urban revitalization.
attractions.hours: District: Open 24/7; Venues: Most bars and restaurants open until 3:00 AM daily
attractions.admission: Free (district entry); Ticket prices vary by event and venue (e.g., College Basketball Experience: $15 adults, $12 kids; Midland Theatre: varies)
highlights: KC Live! Block covered outdoor courtyard hosting 150+ free events annually
Home to more than 50 bars, restaurants, and shops across nine blocks
Features major venues like the T-Mobile Center and Arvest Bank Theatre at The Midland
Interactive sports experiences at The College Basketball Experience
Signature summer concert series like Hot Country Nights and sports watch parties
attractions.tips: Validate your parking ticket at participating venues for discounts in the KC Live! Garage. The KC Streetcar offers free and easy transport between the district and other downtown neighborhoods like River Market and Union Station. Arrive early for concerts at KC Live! as seating is limited and lawn chairs are not permitted.
attractions.nearby: T-Mobile Center, Kauffman Center for the Performing Arts, Crossroads Arts District, Union Station, WWI Museum and Memorial, Science City

attractions.name: Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium (Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium)
attractions.description: The Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium is a 202-acre nature sanctuary in historic Swope Park, home to more than 10,000 animals. It features world-class exhibits like the Sobela Ocean Aquarium and Helzberg Penguin Plaza, connecting people to the natural world through education and conservation. (Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium, Tripadvisor)
attractions.address: 6800 Zoo Drive, Kansas City, MO 64132 (Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium)
attractions.hours: March - October: Mon-Fri 9:30 am - 4 pm, Sat-Sun 9:30 am - 5 pm. November - February: Daily 9:30 am - 4 pm. (Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium)
attractions.days_open: Open Daily (except New Year's Day, MLK Day, Thanksgiving, and Christmas) (Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium)
attractions.admission: Ages 2 and under: FREE. General Admission: $22-$24 online, $25-$28 at gate. Jackson/Clay County Residents: $11-$12 online, $12.50-$14 at gate. FOTZ Guest: $11-$12 online. (Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium)
highlights: Sobela Ocean Aquarium: A 650,000-gallon facility home to nearly 8,000 animals. (Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium)
Helzberg Penguin Plaza: Features four species of penguins and coral reef displays. (Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium)
Africa: A massive section divided into Botswana, Kenya, Tanzania, and more. (Wikipedia)
Polar Bear Passage: An $11 million exhibit with multiple viewing angles and a 140,000-gallon pool. (Wikipedia)
Sky Safari: A chairlift ride offering unique views of the animals from above. (Tripadvisor)
Tropics House: An indoor habitat for primates, otters, and exotic plants. (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Buy tickets online to save money and skip lines. Bring your own food and drinks to avoid high concession prices. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the zoo is very large, and consider renting a scooter for those with mobility issues. Reservations are required for the Sobela Ocean Aquarium. (Tripadvisor, KSHB 41)
attractions.nearby: Starlight Theatre, Lakeside Nature Center, Swope Soccer Village, and Swope Memorial Golf Course. (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Kansas Speedway
attractions.description: Kansas Speedway is a premier 1.5-mile tri-oval race track located in the Village West area. Since its opening in 2001, it has hosted major NASCAR Cup Series weekends, IndyCar races, and various other motor sports events. It also serves as a multi-purpose venue for events like the American Royal World Series of BBQ. (Wikipedia, Visit KC)
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Closed Saturday & Sunday (except on race weekends when gates typically open 90 minutes to 2 hours before the race). (Tripadvisor, Vivid Seats)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Ticket Office); Event-specific weekends for races. (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event. NASCAR Cup Series (e.g., Hollywood Casino 400) tickets typically range from approximately $64 to over $150 for premium seating (Ally Apex Club, Highline Club). Youth tickets (12 and under) often start at $10 for certain events. Some events like the American Royal BBQ may have separate admission. (Official Site, Visit KC)
highlights: Hosts two annual NASCAR Cup Series weekends (AdventHealth 400 and Hollywood Casino 400). (Wikipedia)
Home to the Richard Petty Driving Experience where fans can drive racecars. (Wikipedia)
Features a 48,000-seat capacity grandstand with excellent sightlines. (Wikipedia)
Adjacent to the Hollywood Casino at Kansas Speedway, which overlooks Turn 2. (Official Site)
Hosts the prestigious American Royal World Series of BBQ competition. (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Arrive early on race days as the busiest hours are 8:00 AM to noon. The speedway permits soft-sided coolers (check size limits on site). Download the NASCAR Tracks App for interactive maps and digital ticket management. (Official Site, Visit KC)
attractions.nearby: Hollywood Casino at Kansas Speedway, Legends Outlets Kansas City, Children's Mercy Park (Sporting KC), Nebraska Furniture Mart, Cabela's, Great Wolf Lodge. (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Kauffman Stadium
attractions.description: Kauffman Stadium, affectionately known as "The K," is a premier Major League Baseball ballpark located in Kansas City, Missouri, and serves as the home of the Kansas City Royals. Opened in 1973 as part of the Truman Sports Complex, it is celebrated for its modernist architecture and the spectacular "Water Spectacular" fountains in the outfield. The stadium underwent a significant $250 million renovation in 2009, enhancing fan amenities while preserving its iconic status as one of the few stadiums named after an individual rather than a corporation (Wikipedia, MLB.com).
attractions.address: One Royal Way, Kansas City, MO 64129
attractions.hours: Game Days: Gates open 1 hour (Mon-Thu) or 1.5 hours (Fri-Sun) before first pitch; Guest Services: Open until 60 mins post-game; Business Office: Mon-Fri 9am-5pm (MLB.com)
attractions.days_open: Open for all Royals home games, scheduled public tours, and special events (MLB.com)
attractions.admission: Game tickets: $12 - $77+; Tours: $25 - $65 (Ages 5 and under free); Hall of Fame: Free with game ticket; Parking: $21+ (MLB.com, Tripadvisor)
highlights: Outfield Fountains and Waterfalls: The world's largest privately funded fountains, creating a stunning visual backdrop.
Royals Hall of Fame: An interactive museum celebrating the team's history and the Negro Leagues.
Iconic Crown Scoreboard: A massive, high-definition screen topped with the Royals' signature crown.
Outfield Experience: A family-friendly area featuring mini-golf, a carousel, and the "Little K" youth field.
Buck O'Neil Legacy Seat: A single red seat behind home plate honoring the legendary Negro Leagues player and scout.
Local Culinary Options: Featuring famous Kansas City barbecue from Joe’s KC and other regional favorites. (MLB.com, Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Pre-purchase parking online to save roughly $10 compared to gate prices. Arrive early to visit the Royals Hall of Fame in the outfield, as admission is free with your game ticket. Children 32 inches and under do not require a ticket if they sit on an adult's lap. Download the MLB Ballpark app for digital tickets and the Go-Ahead Entry feature (MLB.com, SeatGeek).
attractions.nearby: GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium, Truman Sports Complex, Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium, National WWI Museum and Memorial (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Kaw Point Park
attractions.description: Kaw Point Park is a 10-acre wooded park located at the confluence of the Kansas and Missouri Rivers, notably serving as the campsite for the Lewis and Clark Expedition in June 1804. The park features beautiful wooded trails, wildflowers, and an outdoor amphitheater, offering some of the most spectacular views of the Kansas City, Missouri skyline. It preserves a natural state within a bustling industrial district and serves as a significant destination on the Lewis and Clark National Historic Trail. (Kaw Point Park, National Park Service)
attractions.hours: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM Daily (Kaw Point Park, Visit Kansas City KS)
attractions.admission: Free (Kaw Point Park, Visit Kansas City KS)
attractions.phone: 913-573-8327 (Unified Government Parks and Recreation) (Wyandotte County Parks)
highlights: Confluence of the Kansas and Missouri Rivers
Site of the 1804 Lewis and Clark Expedition campsite
Stunning panoramic views of the Kansas City, Missouri skyline
Outdoor amphitheater with limestone seating carved with expedition member names
Annual starting point for the MR340, the world's longest nonstop paddling race
Educational pavilion and trailhead for the Lewis and Clark Heritage Trail
attractions.tips: The park is tucked away in an industrial district; follow the signs carefully through the concrete flood wall to find the entrance. It is highly recommended to visit at sunset for excellent photography of the skyline, but avoid staying after the 10:00 PM closing time as vehicles may be locked in. (Kaw Point Park, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Lewis and Clark Heritage Trail, Strawberry Hill Neighborhood, Downtown Kansas City (KS), Missouri Riverfront Heritage Trail (Visit Kansas City KS)

attractions.name: KC Bier Co. (Kansas City Bier Company)
attractions.description: KC Bier Co. is Kansas City's largest locally-owned brewery, specializing in authentic German-style lagers and ales brewed with traditional ingredients and methods. The brewery features a Bavarian-style Bierhalle and an expansive outdoor Biergarten where guests can enjoy fresh, unpasteurized bier alongside classic German fare like pretzels, sausages, and sandwiches.
attractions.hours: Mon: Closed; Tue: 11:00 am–9:00 pm; Wed: 11:00 am–9:00 pm; Thu: 11:00 am–9:00 pm; Fri: 11:00 am–10:00 pm; Sat: 11:00 am–10:00 pm; Sun: 11:00 am–9:00 pm
attractions.admission: Free (Brewery tours: $10; Special events like Art Show: $5-$15)
highlights: Authentic German-style biers brewed with imported German malt and hops
Bavarian-style Bierhalle and family-friendly outdoor Biergarten
Live Polka music and regular community events like Trivia and Music Bingo
Public brewery tours offered on Saturdays and Sundays
Located directly along the historic Harry Wiggins Trolley Track Trail
Extensive menu of German-inspired snacks, sausages, and sandwiches
attractions.tips: Visit during Happy Hour (Tue-Thu, 3-6 PM) for discounts on biers and pretzels. The Biergarten is dog-friendly and family-oriented, featuring a children's playhouse. If you're coming for a tour, it's best to purchase tickets in advance online. Seating is communal in the Biergarten, reflecting traditional German customs.
attractions.nearby: Trolley Track Trail, Waldo Pizza, McLain’s Bakery, Betty Rae’s Ice Cream, Loose Park, The National Museum of Toys and Miniatures

attractions.name: Kemper Museum of Contemporary Art
attractions.description: Opened in 1994, the Kemper Museum of Contemporary Art is Missouri's first and largest contemporary museum, boasting a permanent collection of nearly 1,500 works. The museum is housed in a unique concrete, steel, and glass building designed by architect Gunnar Birkerts, featuring a striking central atrium. It offers free admission and presents 10–12 special exhibitions each year, showcasing a diverse range of modern and contemporary artists. (Wikipedia, Kemper Museum)
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: Closed, Wednesday: 10:00 a.m.–4:00 p.m., Thursday: 10:00 a.m.–9:00 p.m., Friday: 10:00 a.m.–4:00 p.m., Saturday: 10:00 a.m.–4:00 p.m., Sunday: 10:00 a.m.–4:00 p.m. (Kemper Museum)
attractions.admission: Free for all visitors (Kemper Museum)
highlights: A permanent collection of nearly 1,500 works including artists like Louise Bourgeois and Jackson Pollock. (Wikipedia)
The striking museum building designed by architect Gunnar Birkerts, known for its articulated skylight. (Wikipedia)
Café Sebastienne, which integrates fine dining with art, featuring Frederick J. Brown's 'The History of Art' series. (Kemper Museum)
The Atrium Project, an annual series of large-scale, site-specific commissioned installations. (Kemper Museum)
A dynamic schedule of 10–12 special exhibitions each year focusing on global contemporary art. (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Admission and some nearby street parking are free, though the museum's adjacent lot has a $10 fee for non-members. Visitors should check the front desk for large bag storage and note that the museum is fully ADA accessible. Reservations are recommended for the popular Café Sebastienne, especially for Sunday brunch. (Kemper Museum, Audiala)
attractions.nearby: The Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art, Kansas City Art Institute, Ewing and Muriel Kauffman Memorial Garden, and the Country Club Plaza. (Kemper Museum)

attractions.name: Lyric Opera of Kansas City
attractions.description: Lyric Opera of Kansas City is one of the nation’s premier regional opera companies, performing four high-quality live operatic productions each season. Founded in 1958, it is a Grammy-nominated organization known for its artistic excellence and commitment to presenting a diverse repertoire ranging from traditional classics to contemporary American works. (Lyric Opera of Kansas City), (Visit KC)
attractions.address: 1601 Broadway Blvd, Kansas City, MO 64108 (Kauffman Center); 1725 Holmes St., Kansas City, MO 64108 (Box Office)
attractions.hours: Regular Season: Mon-Fri 10:00 am–5:00 pm. Performance Day Hours: Friday 10:00 am–7:30 pm; Saturday 12:00 pm–7:30 pm; Sunday (Closed, but available at venue 90 minutes before curtain). (Lyric Opera of Kansas City)
attractions.days_open: Monday through Friday (Box Office); Performance days vary (typically Friday, Saturday, and Sunday) (Lyric Opera of Kansas City)
attractions.admission: Single tickets range from approximately $39 to $489, with an average price of $215; Season packages range from $105 to $684. (Vivid Seats), (KC Live Theater), (TicketSmarter)
highlights: Resident company at the state-of-the-art Kauffman Center for the Performing Arts. (Visit KC)
Grammy-nominated company recognized for world-class vocal talent and production quality. (Lyric Opera of Kansas City)
Features the "Explorations Series," which presents innovative and intimate performances. (Lyric Opera of Kansas City)
Extensive educational outreach including touring family-friendly operas like "Maya and the Magic Ring." (Lyric Opera of Kansas City)
Mainstage performances take place in the visually stunning 1,800-seat Muriel Kauffman Theatre. (Lyric Opera of Kansas City)
Has a storied legacy of over 65 years since its founding by Russell Patterson in 1958. (Lyric Opera of Kansas City)
attractions.tips: Attend the free Pre-Opera Talks 50 minutes before each mainstage performance to learn about the production. You can pre-order intermission drinks at the bar to skip the lines. The Kauffman Center offers sweeping views of the city from the Brandmeyer Great Hall, so arrive early to explore the venue. (Lyric Opera of Kansas City), (Visit KC)
attractions.nearby: Kansas City Power & Light District, Union Station, National WWI Museum and Memorial, Crossroads Arts District, T-Mobile Center. (Visit KC)

attractions.category: Living History Museum Jackson County Parks + Rec, Wikipedia
attractions.description: Missouri Town Living History Museum is a 30-acre outdoor history museum that recreates a small Missouri town on the eve of the Civil War. It features more than 25 authentic structures from the 1820s to 1860s, filled with historic tools and furnishings to illustrate 19th-century lifestyle. Costumed interpreters, heritage livestock, and heirloom crops help tell the story of daily life in the mid-1800s. Jackson County Parks + Rec, Wikipedia

attractions.name: National Agricultural Center and Hall of Fame Official Website
attractions.category: Museum, Landmark, Educational Facility Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.description: The National Agricultural Center and Hall of Fame is a federally chartered museum and educational facility dedicated to preserving the history of American agriculture. It features extensive collections of antique farm machinery, a replica early 1900s pioneer town, and the National Farmer's Memorial, all aimed at honoring the leadership and impact of the agricultural sector on the nation's heritage Official Website, Wikipedia.
attractions.address: 630 N. 126th Street, Bonner Springs, KS 66012 Official Website
attractions.region: Wyandotte County / Kansas City Metro area Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: Thursday-Saturday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM (Last entry at 1:00 PM) Official Website
attractions.days_open: Thursday, Friday, Saturday (Seasonal: April 25 – October 24) Official Website
attractions.admission: Suggested donation of minimum $5 per person Official Website
highlights: Museum of Farming: A 20,000-square-foot facility housing a vast collection of antique farm machinery and implements Official Website
National Farmer's Memorial: A national monument dedicated to the sacrifices and resilience of American farmers Official Website
Farm Town USA: A replica early 20th-century town featuring a one-room schoolhouse, general store, and blacksmith shop SPAN Magazine
Miniature Narrow-Gauge Train: A popular visitor attraction offering rides around the center's picturesque lake Official Website
National Poultry Museum: An onsite museum located within the Farm Town hatchery SPAN Magazine
Presidential Artifacts: The collection includes the plow used by Harry S. Truman in his youth Official Website
attractions.tips: Plan for at least 2 to 2.5 hours for a self-guided tour. Arrive by 12:30 PM to ensure you have enough time before the early 2:00 PM closing. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the grounds include grassy areas and gravel paths Official Website.

attractions.name: National World War I Museum and Memorial Official Site
attractions.description: The National World War I Museum and Memorial is America's official museum dedicated to remembering, interpreting, and understanding the Great War and its enduring impact on the global community. Located in Kansas City, it features one of the largest WWI collections in the world and the iconic 217-foot Liberty Memorial tower Official Site, Wikipedia.
attractions.address: 2 Memorial Drive, Kansas City, MO 64108 Official Site
attractions.hours: Regular Hours (Labor Day - Memorial Day): Wednesday-Monday 10 a.m. - 5 p.m., Tuesdays CLOSED. Summer Hours (Memorial Day - Labor Day): Daily 10 a.m. - 5 p.m. Official Site
attractions.days_open: Wednesday - Monday (Regular), Daily (Summer: Memorial Day to Labor Day) Official Site
attractions.admission: Adult: $19.50, Senior (65+): $15.50, Youth (6–15): $11.50, Children (Under 6): Free, Military (With ID): $15.50. Additional costs apply for the Liberty Memorial Tower and Featured Exhibitions Ticket Site.
highlights: 217-foot Liberty Memorial Tower offering panoramic views of Kansas City Official Site
A glass bridge entry over a symbolic field of 9,000 red poppies Wikipedia
Extensive collection of over 350,000 artifacts including tanks, uniforms, and weapons Wikipedia
Life-size replica trenches and immersive sound booths Wikipedia
The Over There Café, featuring a WWI-inspired menu Official Site
The Edward Jones Research Center for deep historical study Official Site
attractions.tips: Exhibit galleries close 15-30 minutes before the museum. Purchase a combo ticket to include the Liberty Memorial Tower and Featured Exhibition at a discount. Online ticket booking is recommended for guaranteed entry Official Site.

attractions.name: Oceans of Fun (Worlds of Fun)
attractions.description: Oceans of Fun is a massive 60-acre waterpark in Kansas City, recognized as the largest in the Midwest. It features a wide variety of attractions ranging from high-speed extreme slides like Riptide Raceway to relaxing options like a large wave pool and lazy river. Admission is included with entry to the adjacent Worlds of Fun theme park, offering a complete summer destination for families and thrill-seekers. Oceans of Fun
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 11:00 am - 7:00 pm (Park), Waterpark typically 11:00 am/12:00 pm - 6:00 pm/7:00 pm; Friday: 11:00 am - 7:00 pm (Park); Saturday - Sunday: 11:00 am - 9:00 pm (Park), 11:00 am/12:00 pm - 6:00 pm (Waterpark) Calendar & Hours
attractions.days_open: Open seasonally from late April through summer (operating days vary in May, daily starting late May) Calendar & Hours
attractions.admission: Daily Ticket – Weekday: Starting at $40.99 online ($43 at gate), Daily Ticket – Weekend & Holidays: Starting at $40.99 online ($43 at gate), Daily Ticket – Good Any Day: $55.99 online, 2026 Enchanted Hero Pass: $65.99, 2026 Enchanted Legend Pass: $90.99 (All prices plus applicable taxes & fees; ages 2 and under are Free) Daily Tickets
highlights: Riptide Raceway: The world's longest mat racing waterslide spanning 486 feet.
Hurricane Falls: A 72-foot tall family raft slide with a twisting 680-foot long path.
Surf City Wave Pool: A massive family-friendly pool perfect for swimming and catching waves.
Caribbean Cooler: A relaxing 800-foot lazy river that loops through the park.
Castaway Cove: An exclusive adult-only (21+) area featuring a pool and bar to relax and unwind.
Predators' Plunge: High-intensity slides featuring drop-floor capsules for a vertical start.
attractions.tips: Admission includes access to both Worlds of Fun and Oceans of Fun, so bring a change of clothes and store them in on-site lockers. No outside coolers are allowed, but you can use the picnic area outside the gates and get free re-entry. Visit on Tuesday through Thursday for the shortest wait times. Reddit, Kansas City Mom Collective

attractions.name: Park University
attractions.description: Park University is a private, nonprofit, liberal arts institution founded in 1875. Its flagship 645-acre wooded campus is located in the historic town of Parkville, Missouri, on a bluff overlooking the Missouri River. The university is known for its historic Mackay Hall and its unique commercial and academic underground system housed in former limestone mines.

attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Campus grounds); Monday - Friday (Administrative offices)
attractions.admission: Free (Campus visits and some events are free; tuition applies for students)
highlights: Historic Mackay Hall, built by students in the late 19th century using on-campus mined limestone
Parkville Commercial Underground, a massive subsurface complex for academic and commercial use
Beautiful 700-acre campus overlooking the Missouri River and historic downtown Parkville
Campanella Gallery, which showcases professional and student art exhibits year-round
Extensive military and veteran student services with many campus centers located on military bases
Wide range of student activities including traditional events like Fright Night and Spring Fling
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to explore the historic Mackay Hall and the unique underground campus. For those walking or running on campus, it is recommended to stay on designated paths and sidewalks for safety. Note that visitor hours for tours are typically hourly from 10 am to 3 pm on weekdays, excluding Wednesdays.

attractions.name: Rockhurst University
attractions.description: Rockhurst University is a private Jesuit university in Kansas City, Missouri, founded in 1910. It offers more than 50 undergraduate and graduate programs and is deeply committed to the Ignatian philosophy of educating leadership through service, with 97% of students participating in community service. The university's 55-acre campus is located in the heart of Kansas City’s cultural district, providing a community-centered environment for its students. Rockhurst University, Wikipedia

As the city's largest continually operating performing arts organization, it presents a diverse lineup of Broadway musicals, popular concerts, and community programs (Starlight Official Site).
