attractions.name: Strawberry Hill (Neighborhood) / Strawberry Hill Museum & Cultural Center
attractions.description: Strawberry Hill is a historic neighborhood in Kansas City, Kansas, situated on a bluff overlooking the confluence of the Kansas and Missouri Rivers. Originally settled in the late 19th century by Slavic immigrants who worked in the nearby meatpacking industry, the area remains a vibrant community dedicated to preserving its rich Eastern European heritage and unique architectural character (Wikipedia), Strawberry Hill Neighborhood Association).
attractions.hours: Guided Tours: Saturday & Sunday at 11:30 AM, 1:00 PM, and 2:30 PM (Strawberry Hill Museum)
attractions.days_open: Saturday, Sunday (for museum tours); Public access daily for the neighborhood (Strawberry Hill Museum)
attractions.admission: Adults (12 & Older): $10.00; Children (Ages 6-12): $5.00; Children (Under Age 6): Free (Strawberry Hill Museum)
highlights: Strong Eastern European and Slavic heritage preservation (Visit KCK)
Historic Victorian Queen Anne architecture, notably the Cruise-Scroggs Mansion (Strawberry Hill Museum)
Panoramic views of the Kansas City, Missouri skyline from St. John's Park (LIVINKC)
Historic brick sidewalks and traditional 'front porch' community culture (Strawberry Hill Neighborhood Association)
Renowned local food scene featuring Slap's BBQ and traditional Croatian sausages at Krizman's Sausage (Visit KCK)
Active arts community with repurposed historic buildings like Voler: Thieves of Flight (Visit KCK)
attractions.tips: Reservations are required for museum tours and should be purchased online in advance. Visit the Tea Room on the first Saturday of the month for the New International High Tea. Explore the neighborhood on foot to appreciate the historic brick sidewalks and unique 'shotgun' style houses (Strawberry Hill Museum, Strawberry Hill Neighborhood Association).

attractions.name: Swope Park
attractions.description: Sprawling over 1,805 acres, Swope Park is considered the "crown jewel" of the Kansas City park system and stands as one of the largest municipal parks in the United States. It hosts more than two million visitors annually and serves as a major cultural hub, containing several of the city's most prominent attractions including a world-class zoo, an iconic outdoor theater, and expansive nature trails.

attractions.region: South Kansas City / Swope Park Neighborhood
attractions.hours: 5:00 AM – 12:00 AM (Midnight) every day
attractions.admission: Park Entry: Free. Major Attractions (Pricing Varies): Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium ($22–$24 online; $25–$28 gate; Resident discounts available), Go Ape Treetop Adventure ($54.95–$59.95), Lakeside Nature Center (Free), Starlight Theatre (Varies by performance).
highlights: Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium, home to over 1,000 animals and a world-class African exhibit
Starlight Theatre, a historic 8,000-seat outdoor amphitheater hosting Broadway shows and concerts
Lakeside Nature Center, one of Missouri's largest native species rehabilitation centers
Go Ape Zipline & Adventure Park, featuring high-ropes courses and forest canopy ziplines

Extensive trail system for hiking and mountain biking, including the 5-mile Wudchuck Run loop
attractions.tips: Advance reservations are strongly recommended for popular attractions like Go Ape and Starlight Theatre. For hikers and outdoor adventurers, it is advised to bring plenty of water, wear sturdy enclosed shoes, and stay on marked trails. Visiting the Swope Memorial near the golf course provides one of the best scenic views in the park, especially during the fall.

attractions.name: The National Museum of Toys and Miniatures
attractions.description: The National Museum of Toys and Miniatures houses the world's largest collection of fine-scale miniatures and one of the nation's largest collections of historic toys. Located on the University of Missouri–Kansas City campus, the museum spans over 33,000 square feet and features a collection of more than 100,000 objects. It offers visitors a sense of wonder through intricate art and cultural history, making it a premier destination for families and art enthusiasts alike Official Website, Wikipedia.

attractions.name: Waldo (Neighborhood)
attractions.description: Waldo is a vibrant and diverse neighborhood and business district in South Kansas City, known for its independent spirit and eclectic mix of locally-owned businesses. Originally founded in 1841 as farmland by Dr. David Waldo, it evolved into a major transportation hub and today offers a walkable, bikeable community rich in history and local character.
attractions.address: 7222 Wornall Rd (Business Association Office) / Centered at 75th St & Wornall Rd
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Open 24/7; Business Association Office: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Alexander Majors House: May-Oct Sat-Sun 1:00 PM - 4:00 PM
attractions.admission: Free (Neighborhood); Alexander Majors House Museum: Adults $8, Seniors/Students $6, Children (6-12) $5, Under 6 Free
highlights: Harry Wiggins Trolley Track Trail for walking and biking
Historic Waldo Water Tower (Frank T. Riley Memorial) landmark
Alexander Majors Historic House & Museum, home of the Pony Express founder
Vibrant locally-owned business district with over 600 businesses
Annual community events like the Waldo Fall Festival and Waldo Week
Diverse dining and nightlife scene featuring local favorites like Waldo Pizza and KC Bier Co.
attractions.tips: The neighborhood is best explored on foot or by bike via the Trolley Track Trail. Many of the most popular shops and restaurants are concentrated along Wornall Road between Gregory Blvd and 85th Street. Parking is generally accessible on side streets or in designated business lots.
attractions.nearby: Brookside, Ward Parkway Center, Tower Park, Sunnyside Park, Country Club Plaza

attractions.name: Worlds of Fun
attractions.description: Worlds of Fun is a premier 325-acre amusement destination in the Midwest, featuring international-themed areas inspired by Jules Verne's 'Around the World in Eighty Days.' The park offers a wide variety of attractions, including world-class roller coasters, family-friendly rides, and a massive water park included with admission Official Site.
attractions.address: 4545 Worlds of Fun Ave, Kansas City, MO 64161 Official Site
attractions.region: Northeast Kansas City Kansas City Star
attractions.hours: Friday: 11:00 am - 7:00 pm; Saturday: 11:00 am - 9:00 pm; Sunday: 11:00 am - 9:00 pm; Monday-Thursday: Closed (Typical May schedule) Official Site
attractions.days_open: Friday, Saturday, Sunday (May schedule) Official Site
attractions.admission: Daily Ticket (Good Any Day): $59.99 online ($64 at gate); Weekday Daily Ticket: Starting at $44.99 online; Enchanted Legend Pass: $90.99; Enchanted Hero Pass: $65.99 Official Site
highlights: Zambezi Zinger: A reimagined hybrid steel-and-wood coaster in the Serengeti area Official Site
Mamba: One of the tallest and fastest hyper-coasters in the region Official Site
Patriot: A high-speed inverted coaster with four inversions Official Site
Oceans of Fun: A 60-acre water park featuring the record-breaking Riptide Raceway Official Site
Planet Snoopy: A dedicated Peanuts-themed area with over 20 rides for children Official Site
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable shoes for walking across 325 acres; the park is entirely cashless, so bring a card; visit Mamba and Patriot early to beat crowds; check the chaperone policy for minors Reddit
attractions.nearby: Oceans of Fun, Worlds of Fun Village, Ameristar Casino Kansas City, GEHA Field at Arrowhead Stadium, Penguin Park Tripadvisor
